---
title: Project Init
description: How to create and initialize a new Agent project with the CLI
---

# Project Init

The `downcity agent init` command creates a new Agent project with the minimum files needed to run.

## Basic usage

```bash
downcity agent init my-project
```

This creates:

```text
my-project/
├── downcity.json
├── PROFILE.md
├── SOUL.md
├── .downcity/
└── .env
```

## Generated files

- `downcity.json` — project configuration. See [downcity.json](/en/docs/cli/downcity-json).
- `PROFILE.md` — the agent's visible role and response style. See [Profile](/en/docs/cli/profile).
- `SOUL.md` — deeper, long-term operating principles. See [Profile](/en/docs/cli/profile).
- `.downcity/` — runtime data directory (sessions, logs, cache).
- `.env` — environment variables for the project.

## Options

```bash
# Initialize with a specific template
downcity agent init my-project --template chatbot

# Skip creating default files
downcity agent init my-project --minimal

# Specify a custom directory
downcity agent init my-project --dir ./projects
```

## After init

1. Edit `downcity.json` to set your agent ID and model binding.
2. Edit `PROFILE.md` and `SOUL.md` to shape behavior.
3. Run `downcity agent start` to start the agent.

Continue with:

- [Project Structure](/en/docs/cli/project)
- [downcity.json](/en/docs/cli/downcity-json)
